title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy scalp

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree teas helps with itchy scalp because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which work together to soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's active ingredient, terpinen-4-ol, penetrates deep into the scalp to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness and flakiness. Additionally, tea tree teas' antifungal properties help to reduce dandruff and other scalp irritations, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment. This natural remedy effectively relieves itchy scalp symptoms without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and Antifungal Properties: Tea tree oil, a common ingredient in herbal tea tree teas, has antiseptic and antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections such as dandruff, ringworm, and psoriasis, which can cause itchiness on the scalp.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in tea tree oil and other herbs used in these teas, such as chamomile and calendula, help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from itchy scalp conditions like eczema and psoriasis.

Antioxidant and Immune-Boosting Properties: The antioxidants and immunomodulatory compounds found in herbal tea tree teas, including herbs like peppermint and sage, help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, support immune function, and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating itchiness and promoting a healthy scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Tea tree leaves are a popular choice for medicinal teas due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe an itchy scalp.
Roots: Tea tree roots have been traditionally used to treat skin irritations and infections, including those affecting the scalp, making them a effective ingredient in medicinal teas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia, using scissors or clippers to collect 2-3 tablespoons of foliage.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with cool water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat dry with a paper towel.
Combine the dried or fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves with boiling water in a large teapot, allowing the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid for consumption within an hour.
Drink the tea tree herbal tea three times daily, after meals or as needed, for 1-2 weeks to alleviate itchy scalp symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and itching caused by skin irritations. The tea's antihistamine effects also alleviate allergic reactions, soothing the scalp and providing relief from itchy sensations. Additionally, stinging nettle contains antioxidants that promote healthy hair growth, strengthening the roots and reducing dandruff. As a natural treatment, it's a gentle and effective solution for calming an itchy scalp, leaving your hair looking healthy and vibrant.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Stinging nettle tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress on the scalp, which can contribute to itchiness and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and caffeic acid, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with an itchy scalp.

Antihistamines: The flavonoids present in nettle tea, such as apigenin and luteolin, have antihistamine properties that can help alleviate itchiness and discomfort by blocking histamine receptors on the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Stinging nettle roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated scalps, making them an effective ingredient in teas for itchy scalp relief.
Leaves: Nettle leaves contain a compound called scopoletin, which has antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help combat fungal infections and reduce itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried stinging nettle leaves urtica dioica and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of filtered water and pour it over the dried nettle leaves allowing them to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids completely.
Add two drops of lavender essential oil to the tea as a soothing agent for itchy scalp relief.
Let the tea cool to room temperature and use it as a final rinse after shampooing to soothe itchiness and calm irritation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe teas helps with itchy scalp because its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. The tea's natural astringent and antiseptic properties also help to balance the scalp's pH level, preventing itchiness caused by fungal or bacterial infections. Additionally, aloe vera's moisturizing properties nourish the scalp, removing flakes and scales that can contribute to itchiness, leaving the scalp feeling calm, cool, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness associated with an itchy scalp, providing relief from discomfort.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in aloe vera tea, such as vitamin C and E, can help protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchiness and flakiness.

Sothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's natural soothing and moisturizing properties can calm irritated skin and scalps, reducing itchiness and flaking while promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Barks: Aloe barks have antiseptic properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infections on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from a mature plant ensuring they are free of dirt and debris.
Cut off the top third of each leaf exposing the gel filled inner tissue for use.
Scoop out 20-30 grams of aloe vera gel from the cut leaves into a blender or food processor.
Blend the aloe vera gel with one quart of boiling water for 5 minutes to create a soothing tea mixture.
Strain the blended mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot and serve warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass teas helps with itchy scalp because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea's active compounds, such as citral and geraniol, soothe irritated skin and reduce redness, relieving itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, lemongrass has antibacterial and antifungal properties that help combat dandruff and other scalp conditions, promoting a healthy scalp environment. As the tea is absorbed into the skin, it calms inflammation and reduces flaking, leaving the scalp feeling smooth and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Lemongrass tea's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, reducing itching and flaking caused by conditions like dandruff and ringworm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass tea reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness associated with an itchy scalp, providing relief from irritation and discomfort.

Antioxidant and soothing properties: Lemongrass tea's antioxidant and soothing properties help to calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and stress that can contribute to itching and flaking, while also promoting healthy skin growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated scalps.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of lemongrass contains oils that can help reduce itchiness and inflammation on the scalp, promoting a healthy and flake-free skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemongrass leaves and chop them into small pieces weighing about one ounce or 28 grams.
Combine chopped lemongrass with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup holding at least eight ounces or 237 milliliters.
Steep the mixture for approximately five to seven minutes allowing the essential oils to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea leaves using a fine-mesh sieve and discard any remaining solids leaving behind clear liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down slightly before applying it directly onto the scalp or mixing with other hair care products.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with itchy scalp because its calming properties soothe and relax the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds work to calm the scalp's nerve endings, alleviating irritation and itchiness caused by conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, or eczema. Additionally, lavender's antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal infections that can contribute to an itchy scalp, promoting a healthy and balanced environment for hair growth.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lavender tea helps to combat bacterial infections on the scalp, reducing itchiness and inflammation caused by conditions like dandruff, ringworm, or psoriasis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm irritated skin on the scalp, relieving itching and discomfort associated with conditions like eczema, seborrheic dermatitis, or sunburn.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender tea's antioxidant compounds help to protect the scalp from environmental stressors and oxidative damage, promoting a healthy environment that reduces itchiness and flakiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, lavender roots have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe itchy scalps and promote relaxation.
Seeds: With their high content of fatty acids and antioxidants, lavender seeds help to reduce inflammation and itchiness while promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried english lavender flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground lavender powder with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a separate container and discard the solids, then add honey to taste if desired.
Drink the lavender tea immediately after preparation, ideally three times daily, with a preparation time of approximately 15 minutes per serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's antioxidants also promote healthy blood flow to the scalp, reducing inflammation and itchiness caused by dandruff, psoriasis, or other scalp conditions. Additionally, rosemary's antiseptic properties help to eliminate bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate itchy scalps. By incorporating rosemary teas into your skincare routine, you can achieve a healthy, flake-free scalp that feels refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Antipruritic properties: Rosemary tea's antipruritic properties help to reduce itching and inflammation on the scalp, providing relief from uncomfortable scratching and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, can reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by an itchy scalp conditions like psoriasis, eczema, or dandruff.

Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary tea's antimicrobial agents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that may be contributing to an itchy scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming an itchy scalp.
Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and promote healing on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried rosemarinus officinalis leaves and bring 2 cups of water to boil for steeping.
Measure out 1 tablespoon of dried rosemary leaves or 2 tablespoons of fresh rosemary leaves and add to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the rosemary essence to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the rosemary leaves and discard any solids.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before storing it in an airtight container for up to 3 days or refrigerate immediately for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The tea's antioxidants work to soothe irritated skin, reducing redness and itching caused by conditions like dandruff and psoriasis. Additionally, thyme's natural antimicrobial compounds help combat fungal overgrowth, which can contribute to an itchy scalp. By drinking the tea or applying it topically as a hair rinse, individuals can experience relief from the discomfort and flaking associated with an itchy scalp.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Thyme tea's antifungal properties can help combat fungal infections like ringworm and athlete's foot, which can cause itchiness and flakiness on the scalp.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by skin irritations or allergies that affect the scalp.
Antioxidant properties: Thyme tea's antioxidants can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and damage on the scalp, which can exacerbate itchiness and dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy scalps and reducing redness.
Leaves: The leaves of the thyme plant contain antioxidants and antiseptic compounds, which help to calm irritated skin and reduce dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves or use dried thyme flowers in quantities of one teaspoon per cup.
Heat filtered water to boiling point and pour over the thyme leaves or flowers.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes depending on desired strength of tea.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container.
Allow the cooled tea to be used as a final rinse after shampooing with normal hair care routine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sandalwood teas helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The essential oils present in sandalwood tea, such as alpha-santalol and beta-santalol, have a soothing effect on the scalp, reducing redness and irritation caused by dandruff, psoriasis, or eczema. Additionally, sandalwood tea's antioxidants help to calm down itchiness and inflammation, promoting healthy hair growth and relieving discomfort associated with an itchy scalp. Regular consumption of sandalwood tea can lead to a soothing and calming effect on the scalp, resulting in a healthy and flake-free scalp.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties: Sandalwood tea's antiseptic properties help reduce inflammation and prevent infection, while its anti-inflammatory properties alleviate itchiness and soothe irritated scalps.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Sandalwood tea's antifungal and antibacterial properties combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchy scalp conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and seborrheic dermatitis.
Cooling and calming effects: Sandalwood tea's cooling and calming effects help reduce itchiness, irritation, and redness associated with an itchy scalp, promoting a sense of relaxation and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm an itchy scalp, reducing redness and irritation.
Barks: Contains astringent properties that help to dry out and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating itchy scalps.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried sandalwood powder into a medium-sized saucepan.
Combine the sandalwood powder with four cups of filtered water in the saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass pitcher, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for at least four hours or overnight before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and inflammation caused by conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to itchiness. By drinking pot marigold tea regularly, individuals can experience relief from itchy scalp discomfort and promote a healthy, balanced scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tea helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin conditions, such as eczema and psoriasis, which can cause an itchy scalp.
Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to an itchy scalp, such as dandruff and ringworm.

Soothing and calming properties: Pot marigold tea's calming effects help to reduce irritation and itching caused by dry skin or over-stimulation of the scalp, promoting a sense of relaxation and relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at soothing itchy scalps and reducing redness.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids and carotenoids, which have natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties to calm itchiness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for at least three days.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried calendula officinalis flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the calendula mixture in boiling water for five to seven minutes before straining the liquid with a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine the strained tea with apple cider vinegar at a ratio of two parts tea to one part vinegar and store it in an airtight container.
Use the prepared tea as a final rinse after shampooing and let it sit on your scalp for five to ten minutes before rinsing thoroughly with warm water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with itchy scalp because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The tea's active compounds, such as podophyllotoxin and other lignans, have been shown to reduce inflammation and itching associated with conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and seborrheic dermatitis. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties help to control the growth of Malassezia, a fungus that can contribute to an itchy scalp. By soothing and calming the scalp, Maidenhair tree teas provide relief from persistent itchiness and promote a healthy, flake-free scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis, which can cause an itchy scalp.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants in Maidenhair Tree tea help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate itchiness and irritation, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Cicatrizant properties: Ginkgo biloba's cicatrizant (wound-healing) properties aid in the repair of damaged skin and reduce scarring, which can help soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots are effective in soothing and calming irritated scalps.
Barks: The barks contain tannins and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and itching, making them a popular choice for tea blends.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water for at least 30 seconds.
Combine one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves with one cup boiling filtered water in a tea infuser.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes allowing the active compounds to extract and mingle with the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool down completely before refrigerating it for up to three days or freezing it for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy scalp are a natural and gentle solution to alleviate itchiness, irritation, and flaking on the scalp. These teas work by soothing and calming the skin, reducing inflammation, and promoting healthy hair growth. Examples of herbal teas that help with itchy scalp include peppermint, chamomile, and calendula, which can be brewed and used as a rinse after shampooing or applied topically as a hair mask. By using these teas, individuals can experience relief from itchy scalp symptoms, improved scalp health, and a reduction in stress and discomfort, ultimately enhancing their overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat an itchy scalp with herbal teas is by brewing a mixture of tea leaves and using it as a final rinse after shampooing. Calendula, chamomile, and lavender teas have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe itchy scalps, while peppermint and rosemary teas help to reduce flaking and promote healthy hair growth. Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herbs in boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then strain and use as a final rinse after shampooing. Repeat once or twice a week for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy scalp that are treated with herbal teas are eczema, psoriasis, and acne. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of herbs such as chamomile, calendula, and tea tree can soothe irritated skin, reduce redness, and promote healing. These teas may be used as a warm compress or added to bath water for a relaxing and therapeutic experience.
